meta.title: Mercedes GLS X167 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.h1: Mercedes GLS X167 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation GLS full-size luxury SUV — from the M256 straight-six petrol to the M176 V8, the M177 AMG V8, and the OM656 straight-six diesel that powers the GLS 400 d. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the 2023 facelift's engine updates explained. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes GLS X167 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X167 GLS specifically — including pre-facelift M256/M176 units, the 2023 facelift updates, and the range-topping AMG and Maybach variants.

overview.intro: The X167 is the third-generation Mercedes-Benz GLS, the flagship full-size luxury SUV produced since October 2019. It replaces the X166 GLS and is built on the MHA platform (a variant of the MRA architecture). The engine range spans M256 straight-six petrols with EQ Boost mild-hybrid technology, M176 V8 petrols in the GLS 580, M177 V8s in the AMG GLS 63 and Maybach GLS 600, plus OM656 straight-six diesels. A 2023 facelift brought power increases and updated engines.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes MHA (Modular High Architecture) • Predecessor: Mercedes-Benz GLS (X166) • Successor: None (current generation) • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (GLS is a standalone model).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The X167 GLS generated UK engine enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA], with the M256 petrol six and OM656 diesel six remaining the most commonly discussed among specialists.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — one paragraph: The X167's most dependable engine is the OM656 2.9-litre straight-six diesel (GLS 400 d, 330 PS) — Mercedes' latest-generation straight-six diesel, offering effortless torque, excellent refinement, and proven durability. The M256 3.0-litre straight-six petrol (GLS 450, 367 PS) is the best petrol option — smooth, refined, and with EQ Boost mild-hybrid assistance adding 22 PS and 250 Nm of torque. The M176 V8 in the GLS 580 (489 PS) offers effortless V8 performance but carries significantly higher running costs. The M177 V8 in the AMG GLS 63 (612 PS) is a masterpiece — epic performance but eye-watering maintenance bills. Avoid any car without documented service history, and be cautious of the V8 engines' cooling systems — the hot-vee design generates significant heat that can affect ancillaries.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — smooth straight-six, proven M256

rows.commonMajorFailure: Cooling system, valve stem seals, engine mounts
rows.ownershipVerdict: V8 Power — effortless but costly to maintain
Facelift V8 — more power, same caveats

rows.commonMajorFailure: Valve stem seals, coolant pipes, engine mounts
rows.ownershipVerdict: AMG Icon — epic engine, epic bills

rows.commonMajorFailure: AdBlue system, EGR cooler, injector wear

rows.ownershipVerdict: Modern Four — efficient but less torque

economicsRule.title: The X167 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,500–£6,000 recon on a sub-£30,000 X167 sits at 12–20% of the car's value — viable on most examples. Against a £10,000 engine bill on a GLS 580, the car's value determines the decision — a £50,000 GLS 580 justifies a £6,000–£10,000 recon; a £30,000 example makes it a much harder call. For an AMG GLS 63, a £70,000+ car justifies a £12,000–£18,000 recon. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line — most X167s sit comfortably above it.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X167 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLS 350 d (OM656) — proven straight-six diesel, excellent economy, cheapest to maintain.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X167 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: AMG GLS 63 (M177) — V8 complexity, specialist-only work, eye-watering parts prices.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X167 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLS 450 (M256.930) — 367 PS straight-six, reliable, refined, without V8 running costs.

problems.title: M256 Straight-Six 48V Battery & Cooling System Issues
problems.rootCause: The M256's 48V mild-hybrid system — including the lithium-ion battery, integrated starter generator (ISG), and cooling system — can develop issues. The water pump and 48V battery are the most common failure points, with the battery typically requiring replacement around 80,000–100,000 miles. The system's complexity means specialist diagnostics are essential.

tieredCosts.work: 48V battery health check, water pump replacement
Full 48V system overhaul including ISG and battery

problems.recommendation: Have the 48V battery and cooling system inspected at every service after 60,000 miles — £700–£1,200 in preventative work is cheap insurance against the £2,800+ bill that follows if the system fails completely. Use the correct Mercedes-approved 48V battery and coolant spec.

cta.label: Compare M256 engine prices for your X167 GLS →

problems.title: M176/M177 V8 Cooling System & Valve Stem Seal Wear
problems.affectedModels: X167 GLS 580 (M176 V8), AMG GLS 63 (M177 V8), Maybach GLS 600 (M177 V8).
problems.rootCause: The M176 and M177's hot-vee design — turbos located in the V of the engine — generates significant heat, which can cause cooling system components (water pump, thermostat housing, coolant pipes) to fail prematurely. Valve stem seals can also harden and fail, leading to oil consumption and blue smoke on startup.

tieredCosts.work: Replace water pump, thermostat, and coolant pipes
Full engine rebuild if valve stem seal failure has caused damage

problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and look for signs of leaks around the water pump and thermostat housing — £1,000–£1,800 in preventative work is cheap insurance against the £5,000+ bill that follows an overheat. If your V8 is smoking on startup or using oil between services, have the valve stem seals inspected immediately.

cta.label: Compare M176/M177 engine prices for your X167 GLS →

problems.title: OM656 Diesel AdBlue & EGR System Issues
problems.affectedModels: X167 GLS 350 d, GLS 400 d (OM656 diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The OM656's AdBlue system — including NOx sensors, heaters, and the EGR cooler — can fail, triggering warning lights and reduced performance. This is common on cars used for short urban journeys where the AdBlue system doesn't complete its regeneration cycles.

tieredCosts.work: NOx sensor replacement, AdBlue system flush
Full AdBlue system overhaul including EGR cooler

problems.recommendation: Use the car regularly and give it a good motorway run to keep the AdBlue and EGR systems clear — £400–£800 in preventative work is cheap insurance against the £1,800+ bill that follows if the system fails completely. Use premium diesel and quality AdBlue.

cta.label: Compare OM656 engine prices for your X167 GLS diesel →

problems.title: X167 Air Suspension System Failure
problems.rootCause: The X167's Airmatic air suspension system — including air springs, compressors, and valve blocks — can develop leaks and fail over time. While not an engine failure, it's one of the most common X167 enquiries and a significant ownership cost for a 2.4-tonne luxury SUV.

tieredCosts.work: Replace air springs and check compressor
Full air suspension overhaul including compressor and valve block

problems.recommendation: Have the air suspension inspected at every service after 60,000 miles — £600–£1,200 in preventative work is a fraction of the £2,000+ bill if the compressor fails and damages the valve block. Listen for the compressor running excessively — it's a sign of a leak.

cta.label: Compare X167 air suspension repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: X167 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are X167 GLS only. The GLS is a standalone model with no sibling body styles in this generation. The X167 was launched in October 2019, with a 2023 facelift bringing updated power outputs and styling changes. The Maybach GLS 600 is the ultra-luxury variant.
